course_subtitle: In this module, you will explore the practical applications of the Open Rack v3 (ORv3) standard, focusing on real-world implementations by industry leaders such as Meta and Google.
course_description_html: In this module, you will explore the practical applications of the Open Rack v3 (ORv3) standard, focusing on real-world implementations by industry leaders such as Meta and Google. You will gain insights into navigating the ORv3 Marketplace, discovering technical contributions from the Open Compute Project community, and evaluating various ORv3-compatible products to determine the best fit for your deployment needs. This comprehensive overview will equip you with the knowledge necessary to make informed decisions in your own ORv3 adoption journey.

blocks.body_html: By the end of this module, you’ll be able to:

items_html: Identify real-world ORv3 adoption examples, including implementations by Meta and Google
Navigate the ORv3 Marketplace and explore contributions from the OCP community
Evaluate and compare ORv3-compatible products to choose the right implementation for your needs

blocks.body_html: Together with Rittal, Meta has contributed a technical specification for a specific implementation of ORv3. This so-called Meta Version of Open Rack Frame V3 builds on the OCP base specifications of ORv3. In 2023, Meta and Rittal presented how this specific evolution over Meta’s previous ORv2 creates many improvements.

Over time, Meta’s implementation of ORv3 continues to evolve their own open contribution as well as help update the base specification. In 2024, Meta continues to work on high power racks (covered in the next course module) and ORv3 Networking (video, presentation).

Meta’s ORv3 frame, developed with Rittal, is a hardened implementation of the OCP standard built for scale, serviceability, and consistency across global data centers. The rack supports 21” IT gear and up to 1,400kg payloads, with optimized airflow and front-access servicing.

When we show up to the present moment with all of our senses, we invite the world to fill us with joy. The pains of the past are behind us. The future has yet to unfold. But the now is full of beauty simply waiting for our attention.
Meta continues to refine its ORv3 design through real-world use and contributions to the broader specification.

blocks.label: CONTINUE
blocks.complete_hint: Complete the content above before moving on.

blocks.body_html: Google’s implementation of ORv3 adapts the base specification for mixed-environment deployments, supporting diverse workloads and hardware configurations. The design focuses on mechanical flexibility, high load capacity, and broad equipment compatibility—while preserving OCP standards.
Click on the key features to learn more.

items.title: Mixed Payload Support
items.description_html: Accommodates 21” OCP gear and EIA 19” equipment

items.title: Flexible Power
items.description_html: Designed for both 48V DC busbars and rear AC PDUs

items.title: Accessory-Ready
items.description_html: Allows front and rear accessories, cable management, and optional EMI containment

items.title: Wide Payload Support
items.description_html: Accepts gear up to 24” wide and over 36” deep, enabling high-performance compute configurations

blocks.heading_html: Google’s ORv3 Rack Design

blocks.body_html:
Design Philosophy:

Google’s rack design balances OCP compliance with the need to integrate legacy systems, custom gear, and emerging AI hardware. It demonstrates how ORv3 can evolve to support real-world deployment needs beyond a single “standard” rack.
Check out the presentation of Google's ORv3 implementation.

blocks.label: CONTINUE
blocks.complete_hint: Complete the content above before moving on.

blocks.body_html: The OCP Marketplace portal and Community Contributions Database make it easy to explore available ORv3-compatible hardware and track ongoing innovation. These resources help organizations evaluate and adopt ORv3 solutions with confidence, leveraging collective expertise from across the industry.

items_html: Certified ORv3 products (racks, power shelves, busbars, accessories)

media.alt: OCP Marketplace results filtered for Open Rack v3 products

blocks.body_html:
Together, the Community Contributions and Marketplace help ORv3 evolve with AI, sustainability, and global scaling in mind.

Marketplace / Community

blocks.label: CONTINUE

blocks.body_html: Choosing the right ORv3 rack isn’t one-size-fits-all. Different priorities—like power density, sustainability, or ease of deployment—demand tailored solutions. In this interactive scenario, you’ll evaluate two ORv3 rack options and decide which is best for a real-world use case.
You’re a solution architect evaluating two ORv3 rack designs for a new global data center rollout.
Each site will deploy 100 racks to support AI workloads at scale. Your priorities are:

items_html: Readiness for liquid cooling and high-density compute

blocks.body_html: You have two choices:

items_html: Rack A: Optimized for modular cooling and 21” OCP gear
Rack B: Supports mixed 21”/19” gear with rear power delivery for legacy integration

blocks.body_html: Which is the better fit for your deployment?
Check the comparison between Rack A and Rack B.

Feature

Rack A : AI-Optimised

Rack B: Legacy-Compatible

Power Delivery

Front 48V DC (standardized OCP spec Option 1)

Rear AC (supports legacy gear)

Cooling Readiness

Blind-mate liquid cooling connectors supported

No integrated liquid cooling features

Airflow

Consistent front-to-back, simplified service design

More complex routing needed to maintain airflow

IT Gear Support

21” OCP gear

21” and 19” EIA gear

Scalability

Standardised across global sites

Customization may be required for mixed gear

Certifications

Fully OCP certified, based on Meta/Google reference designs

Certification varies by vendor

blocks.label: CONTINUE
blocks.complete_hint: Complete the content above before moving on.

blocks.question_html: Which ORv3 rack design would you choose for the scenario above?
blocks.feedback_correct_html: Correct! Rack A is the optimal choice for a 100-rack, AI-ready global facility. It provides standardization across locations, front-facing power delivery, readiness for liquid cooling, and full alignment with the OCP ecosystem.
blocks.feedback_incorrect_html: Not quite. Rack A is the optimal choice for a 100-rack, AI-ready global facility. It provides standardization across locations, front-facing power delivery, readiness for liquid cooling, and full alignment with the OCP ecosystem.

blocks.label: CONTINUE
blocks.complete_hint: Complete the content above before moving on.

blocks.body_html: Test your knowledge from this module. Select the best answer for each question, then submit.

blocks.question_html: What is a key design feature of Meta’s ORv3 implementation?
blocks.feedback_correct_html: Correct! Meta uses standardized 21" gear and airflow profiles to enable consistent, scalable deployments across sites.
blocks.feedback_incorrect_html: Not quite. Meta uses standardized 21" gear and airflow profiles to enable consistent, scalable deployments across sites.

blocks.label: CONTINUE
blocks.complete_hint: Complete the content above before moving on.

blocks.question_html: Which company’s ORv3-compatible rack implementation includes options for 32” and 48” depth and supports payloads up to 3,000 lbs?
blocks.feedback_correct_html: Correct! Google’s ORv3 implementation includes flexible depth options (32" and 48") and supports payloads up to 3,000 lbs, enabling a wide range of IT gear, including 19” legacy systems.
blocks.feedback_incorrect_html: Not quite. Google’s ORv3 implementation includes flexible depth options (32" and 48") and supports payloads up to 3,000 lbs, enabling a wide range of IT gear, including 19” legacy systems.

blocks.label: CONTINUE
blocks.complete_hint: Complete the content above before moving on.

blocks.question_html: Where can you explore recognized ORv3-compatible racks, power shelves, and supplier data?
blocks.feedback_correct_html: Correct! The OCP Marketplace lists verified ORv3 hardware, supplier contacts, and spec details.
blocks.feedback_incorrect_html: Not quite. The OCP Marketplace lists verified ORv3 hardware, supplier contacts, and spec details.

blocks.label: CONTINUE
blocks.complete_hint: Complete the content above before moving on.
